title: Best Cheap VPNs Under €3/Month That Are Actually Good
description: Not free VPNs, not scammy services. These are real paid VPNs priced under or near €3/month on long-term plans, tested for privacy and performance.
content:
## The Budget VPN Problem

Searching for a cheap VPN produces two kinds of results: free services that monetize your data instead of your wallet, and paid services with misleading pricing that shows the discounted two-year rate upfront and hides the renewal cost in small print.

This guide covers real paid options where the discounted price is still worth paying even after the first billing cycle. All of these are legitimate services, not data brokers in disguise.

## Why Free VPNs Are Usually the Wrong Choice

Free VPNs have to make money somehow. The common methods include: selling anonymized (or not-so-anonymized) browsing data to advertisers, injecting tracking cookies, throttling speeds to push you toward paid tiers, and in some cases selling bandwidth (as Hola famously did, turning users into exit nodes for others' traffic).

A paid VPN that costs €2/month is not expensive. It costs less than a cup of coffee. The business model is straightforward: you pay, they provide the service. Free VPNs have misaligned incentives by design.

The one exception worth mentioning: **ProtonVPN's free tier** has no data cap, no speed throttling beyond server selection limits, and has passed independent audits. It is genuinely free without monetizing your traffic. The tradeoff is limited server selection (a few countries) and slower speeds during peak hours.

## VPNs Under or Near €3/Month

### Surfshark — approximately €1.99–2.49/month

Surfshark regularly runs promotions that bring a 24-month plan to around $1.99/month (roughly €1.85 at current rates). The key features that make it worth considering at this price:

- Unlimited simultaneous connections (most VPNs cap at 5–10 devices)
- No-logs policy independently verified by Deloitte in June 2025
- Cure53 and SecuRing security audits on record
- NoBorders mode for use in restrictive regions
- Nexus architecture routes traffic through multiple nodes

**The catch:** Surfshark's parent company Nord Security also owns NordVPN. Both operate independently with separate infrastructure, but they share corporate ownership. For most users this is irrelevant; for users who want fully independent providers, it is worth knowing.

**Renewal pricing** is significantly higher than the promotional rate. Factor that in before committing to a two-year plan you intend to renew.

### ProtonVPN — approximately €2.99/month on a 24-month plan

Proton is at the top of the sub-€3 range rather than comfortably inside it, but it deserves inclusion because the value proposition is different from the others.

Proton AG is a Swiss non-profit foundation structure. The VPN business funds the broader Proton mission of providing private communications tools. ProtonVPN has:

- Four consecutive annual Securitum no-logs audits (2022–2025)
- SOC 2 Type II certification (July 2025)
- Secure Core multi-hop routing through Switzerland and Iceland
- RAM-only servers
- Full open-source apps with reproducible builds
- A genuinely usable free tier as a fallback

For privacy-conscious users who want verified no-logs protection and are willing to pay at the top of the budget bracket, ProtonVPN is the strongest option in this price range.

### NordVPN — approximately €3.09/month on a 2-year plan

NordVPN just clears the €3 threshold on its longest-term promotional pricing. It is included here because the per-month cost is close enough to matter, and the feature set is strong:

- Sixth Deloitte no-logs audit completed December 2025 using ISAE 3000
- RAM-only servers following the 2018–2019 incident
- Threat Protection Pro blocks ads, trackers, and malicious domains
- Meshnet for encrypted device-to-device connections
- Split tunneling available on Windows and Android
- 6,000+ servers across 111 countries

NordVPN is a primary recommendation on this site. The combination of a deep audit record, fast servers, and strong apps makes it the safest bet at this price point if you want a set-it-and-forget-it VPN.

### Bitdefender VPN — approximately €2.50/month on an annual plan

Bitdefender's VPN is less often discussed in privacy circles because it is primarily a security-suite add-on rather than a standalone privacy product. The underlying technology is Hotspot Shield's Catapult Hydra protocol.

At €2.50/month it is cheap, but the privacy story is weaker than the others here: Hotspot Shield has a mixed history with data collection, and Bitdefender's audit history for the VPN specifically is thinner. It is fine for streaming and general security on public Wi-Fi. It is not the right tool for privacy-critical use.

## What to Watch Out For

**Introductory pricing vs. renewal pricing.** A VPN advertised at €1.99/month is often €9.99/month after the first two-year period. Set a calendar reminder before renewal. Some providers offer a 30-day money-back guarantee that can be used as a trial period.

**Simultaneous connection limits.** If you have 4–5 devices, a provider with a 10-device limit is fine. Surfshark's unlimited connections policy makes it easier if you want to cover your whole household.

**App quality on your platform.** Linux support and router firmware vary significantly. ProtonVPN and NordVPN both have strong Linux clients. Surfshark's Linux app is command-line only as of mid-2026.

## Recommendation

For the best value under €3/month: **Surfshark** if you need unlimited devices and a low upfront price. **ProtonVPN** if privacy verification and Swiss jurisdiction matter to you. **NordVPN** if you want the deepest audit record and a well-rounded app at just above €3/month.

All three are real, audited, paid services. None monetize your data. The differences are in features, audit depth, and corporate structure rather than in basic trustworthiness.
